title: A single issue exist on two boards
excerpt:
I’m a bit confused on how mirroring/connect boards will or will not work with my use case. It seems simple and straight forward in theory, but the implementation – maybe not?

posts.author: cire12 (Eric Powelson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m a bit confused on how mirroring/connect boards will or will not work with my use case. It seems simple and straight forward in theory, but the implementation – maybe not?

We have individual development boards for each hardware device that we manufacture:

  • Model A
  • Model B
  • Model C

Issues opened in each of these boards have a field (currently a label) related to a specific set of operating environments:

  • Environment 1
  • Environment 2
  • Environment 3

Each of these operating environments have their own board.

When an issue in the individual model boards is tagged for a specific environment, that issue – that exact record – should be represented on the Environment board. It cannot be a copy of the issue, it has to be the same record, such that even comments are identical between the two issues.

Is this possible?

Please no YAPPs (Yet Another Paid Plugin)

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: cire12 (Eric Powelson)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah, can’t edit a post… it’s Work Management.

posts.author: KirstyM (Kirsty)
posts.cookedHtml: This is great Desiree, is the commenting in the columns a standard feature for mirrored columns etc, or have you set something up to make that happen? (I’m enterpirse)

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re talking about when you click on the ‘connected’ item and then comment there? It’s just part of Connected/mirrored columns.
